title: OpenAI Launches $14 Billion Deployment Company to Embed AI in Enterprises
summary: OpenAI formed the OpenAI Deployment Company, a standalone business backed by $4 billion in investment, to help organizations build, test, and deploy AI systems tailored to their critical operations.
content: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company on May 11, 2026, a new entity designed to help organizations embed AI into their most important workflows through a specialized team of Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs). The company received $4 billion in investments and carries a $10 billion pre-money valuation, giving it a total implied value of $14 billion backed by 19 leading global investment partners. As part of the launch, OpenAI agreed to acquire Tomoro, an applied AI consulting firm that works with Tesco, Virgin Atlantic, and Supercell, which will bring approximately 150 experienced engineers to the new company from day one. The OpenAI Deployment Company is majority-owned and controlled by OpenAI, ensuring customers receive a unified experience across research, product, and deployment teams. Its FDEs will work closely with business leaders to identify high-impact opportunities, redesign workflows around AI, and build durable systems designed to improve as new models and capabilities come online.

title: Nvidia Partners with David Silver's Ineffable Intelligence to Build Reinforcement Learning AI
summary: Nvidia announced an engineering-level partnership with Ineffable Intelligence, a London-based AI lab founded by former Google DeepMind reinforcement learning chief David Silver, to build AI systems that learn from experience rather than human data.
content: Nvidia announced a partnership with Ineffable Intelligence on May 13, 2026, to co-design infrastructure for large-scale reinforcement learning systems that move beyond the dominant paradigm of training on human-generated data. The London-based startup, founded in late 2025 by UCL professor David Silver — the former lead of DeepMind's reinforcement learning team — raised a record $1.1 billion seed round in April, backed by Sequoia, Lightspeed, Nvidia, Google, and the U.K.'s Sovereign AI Fund. Silver argued that researchers have largely solved the easier problem of AI — building systems that know what humans already know — and the harder challenge is building systems that discover new knowledge independently through experience. Engineers from both companies will collaborate to build a reinforcement learning pipeline at scale using Nvidia's Grace Blackwell chips and Vera Rubin platform. Jensen Huang described the partnership's goal as building "superlearners — systems that learn continuously from experience," marking a potential architectural shift away from the transformer-plus-RLHF paradigm that has defined the past five years.

title: Meta Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI Chat That Even Meta Cannot Read
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced Incognito Chat, which the company calls the first major AI product with end-to-end encryption so conversations leave no server logs and are unreadable even by Meta itself.
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced Incognito Chat on May 13, 2026, positioning it as "the first major AI product where there is no log of your conversations stored on servers." Unlike incognito modes in ChatGPT and Claude — which still retain data for up to 30 days — Meta's version uses end-to-end encryption so that "no one, not even Meta" can read the conversations. Google's Gemini keeps temporary chat data for up to 72 hours, while OpenAI and Anthropic retain incognito sessions for at least 30 days. The feature is built on Private Processing, the same infrastructure Meta launched last year for WhatsApp, and will roll out over the coming months in both WhatsApp and the Meta AI app. The announcement arrives as AI chat logs have become legally significant — ChatGPT conversations are central to lawsuits over mass shootings, and a court has ordered OpenAI to retain conversations indefinitely — making Meta's privacy-first architecture both a product differentiator and a liability hedge.

title: Google Identifies First AI-Developed Zero-Day Exploit, Stopping Planned Mass Attack
summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group identified what it believes to be the first zero-day exploit built with AI, disrupted a criminal group's planned mass exploitation event, and documented state-sponsored actors from China, North Korea, and Russia using AI for cyberattacks at industrial scale.
content: Google's Threat Intelligence Group published a landmark report on May 12, 2026, documenting the first zero-day exploit it believes was built with an AI model — a Python script bypassing two-factor authentication whose hallucinated CVSS scores and structured educational docstrings are characteristic of large language model output. Google discovered the vulnerability before it was deployed, patched it with the affected vendor, and disrupted the criminal group's planned mass exploitation event. The report also documents state-sponsored actors using AI at industrial scale: North Korea's APT45 sent thousands of prompts to recursively analyze CVEs and build exploit arsenals; Chinese groups used a distilled database of 85,000 real-world vulnerabilities to steer AI toward identifying logic flaws traditional scanners miss; and Russia-nexus actors deployed AI-generated decoy code against Ukrainian targets. An Android malware called PROMPTSPY was found using Google's own Gemini API to autonomously navigate victim devices, capture biometric data, and block its own uninstallation. Google's defensive AI agent Big Sleep — developed by DeepMind and Project Zero — found the zero-day before attackers could deploy it, illustrating how both offensive and defensive AI are now operating at machine speed.

title: SAP Unveils Business AI Platform and Autonomous Enterprise Vision at Sapphire 2026
summary: At SAP Sapphire in Orlando, CEO Christian Klein launched the SAP Business AI Platform and SAP Autonomous Suite — a unified architecture of over 200 AI agents designed to run core business operations end-to-end.
content: At SAP Sapphire 2026, CEO Christian Klein unveiled the SAP Business AI Platform, a new foundation that unifies SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation into a single governed environment for building and deploying enterprise AI. At its core is the SAP Knowledge Graph, which gives AI agents a structured map of business data, end-to-end processes, and relationships across a company's SAP landscape, addressing what Klein called the root cause of failed enterprise AI: lack of business context. Building on the platform, SAP launched the SAP Autonomous Suite with more than 200 agents and 50 Joule Assistants spanning five domains — Autonomous Finance, Autonomous Spend, Autonomous Supply Chain, Autonomous HCM, and Autonomous CX. A new SAP AI Agent Hub provides a centralized command center to discover, manage, and govern all agents, SAP and non-SAP, with general availability planned for Q3. SAP also announced a €100 million partner fund, new integrations with Anthropic, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and NVIDIA, and a contractual commitment within RISE with SAP for customers to activate three Joule Assistants within their first year.

title: Gallup: Half of U.S. Workers Now Use AI at Work as Adoption Reshapes Staffing
summary: For the first time in Gallup's tracking, 50% of employed American adults report using AI in their role at least several times a year, with AI-adopting organizations showing simultaneously higher rates of both hiring and layoffs than peers.
content: Gallup's February 2026 survey of 23,717 U.S. employees found that for the first time, half of employed Americans use AI in their role at least a few times a year, up from 46% the previous quarter, with 13% using it daily. Forty-one percent of employees now work in organizations that have formally integrated AI tools, up three points from the prior quarter. While 65% of employees in AI-adopting organizations say AI has improved their productivity, only about one in ten strongly agree it has transformed how work gets done at the organizational level — suggesting most gains are concentrated at the individual task level rather than through redesigned workflows. Workers in AI-adopting companies are more likely to report both workforce expansion (34% vs. 28%) and reductions (23% vs. 16%) compared to non-AI organizations, indicating that AI amplifies staffing volatility in both directions. Eighteen percent of all U.S. employees say it is likely their job will be eliminated within five years due to AI — rising to 23% among those at companies that have already adopted AI.
